channel.title: OnOptions
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from OnOptions, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Whiskey Is Struggling. Jim Beam’s Owner Has a Surprising Backup Plan.
item.description: Soft drinks, canned cocktails, and non-alcoholic beverages are offsetting weaker spirits demand.

item.title: This new REI short film might inspire you to run, but it will definitely make you cry
item.description: Rob Shaver is a 49-year-old retail worker who recently had a streak of running at least 1 mile every day for three years. He’s also been living with S...

item.title: Why Amazon Founder Jeff Bezos Says the Answer to This Question is the Surest Path to Business Success
item.description: Sure, be innovative… but first, focus on things that won’t change.

item.title: How to thrive in the era of the ‘supermanager’
item.description: If you’re a manager today, your job may well be changing. That is, if it hasn’t already. As companies continue to compress their org charts and axe la...

item.title: Why Amazon Is Lagging the Magnificent 7 Stocks
item.description: Wall Street is worried about its $200 billion spending plans for the year.

item.title: The first-ever Snoopy set is Lego perfection
item.description: Recently, I made myself a promise: I would not buy any more Lego for at least a year. That plan has quickly been foiled. Lego’s first-ever Peanuts set...

item.title: Are women board members risk averse or agents of innovation?
item.description: Do women board members make a company more innovative or risk-averse? The answer is both, according to our recent study . It all depends on how the co...

item.title: Nike Was One Bad Phone Call Away From Bankruptcy. Here’s How It Survived.
item.description: The brand had to face a cash-flow challenge to become the iconic company we know today.

item.title: Distrust and disempowerment prevent allyship in the workplace, research shows
item.description: What really holds people back from stepping up as allies in support of their marginalized colleagues? For example, why don’t more men say something wh...

item.title: Stop Trying to Please Everyone. The Businesses That Win Do This Instead
item.description: Some businesses are born from opportunity. Others are born from conviction.

item.title: Trump crackdown drives 80% plunge in immigrant employment, reshaping labor market, Goldman says
item.description: Net immigration averaged approximately 1 million people per year during the 2010s. Not anymore.

item.title: Why your boss loves AI and you hate it: Corporate profits are capturing your extra productivity, and your salary isn’t
item.description: As happened with the first factories of the Industrial Revolution, today’s AI boom is boosting output, but most of the gains are flowing to capital, n...

item.title: CEO hopefuls have a new rival for the top job: their own board directors
item.description: Internal candidates are increasingly up against their own directors in the CEO succession race.

item.title: Thousands of CEOs just admitted AI had no impact on employment or productivity—and it has economists resurrecting a paradox from 40 years ago
item.description: In the 1980s, economist Robert Solow made an observation that reminded economists of today’s AI boom: “You can see the computer age everywhere but in ...

item.title: Billionaire Trump supporter blocks sale of Texas warehouse for use as ICE jail
item.description: Majestic Realty is one of several companies to say no DHS after community backlash.
